title: Production swarm ModelSessionFactory and app-state wiring
origin: Authored as a missing MT-203 follow-on contract. MT-203 preserves the explicit follow-on intent: 'MT-204 ModelRuntimeState multi-instance + production ModelSessionFactory (real CandleRuntime load + record_spawn + teardown=detach/drop)'. The operator narrowed this repair scope to production factory/app wiring/operator orchestration, VM-local->cloud escalation, persistent VM warm streaming + restored-snapshot warm-start, and distillation/trace follow-on only where MT-203 explicitly preserved it.
goal: Turn the MT-203 SwarmCoordinator from a hardened backend primitive into a production-loadable local model session runtime by wiring a real ProductionModelSessionFactory to ModelRuntimeState, local runtime loaders, process-ledger START/STOP metadata, Flight Recorder swarm events, and Tauri app state. This MT owns the backend/app-state factory seam only; operator-facing controls are MT-205.

base_scope.summary: Implement the production factory and app-state wiring needed for local model swarm sessions to create, attach, observe, and tear down real local model runtimes through the MT-203 coordinator.
included: Add or complete ProductionModelSessionFactory wiring for local CandleRuntime, llama.cpp runtime, and sandboxed Tier3 microVM runtime where the selected SpawnRequest requires it.
Connect ModelRuntimeState multi-instance attach/detach to the SwarmCoordinator session lifecycle so every spawned model instance has exactly one live runtime owner and teardown frees it.
Thread the factory into app/Tauri runtime state so production commands can use the same coordinator/factory path as tests.
Persist process-ledger START/STOP rows with model_id, instance_id, swarm_id, worktree_id, runtime_binding, provider, sandbox_adapter_id, sandbox_internal_id where available.
Emit durable swarm lifecycle events through the existing SwarmEventSink and Flight Recorder bridge instead of stderr-only reporting.
Keep local production load/unload behavior from MT-202 intact and reuse its command/runtime registration decisions rather than creating a parallel loader.
non_goals: No product feature worktree edits from this governance repair session.
No operator board, chat, or UI controls in this MT; those belong to MT-205.
No routing policy semantics beyond exposing factory errors and overload signals for MT-206.
No persistent warm VM streaming implementation; MT-207 owns that selected follow-on.

expected_diff_shape: Product implementation should touch swarm_orchestration production factory/state modules, model_runtime production loader seams, process-ledger metadata wiring, app/src-tauri state construction, and focused tests. The governance repair only authors this contract.
reuse: MT-202 production model-load/unload flow and ModelRuntimeState attach_live_runtime/detach_live_runtime.
MT-203 SwarmCoordinator, ModelSessionFactory, LiveSession, SessionTeardown, ClaimLease, breaker, budget, and SwarmEventSink.
VM_WORKTREE_SWARM_RESEARCH_BASIS existing swarm_orchestration, process_ledger, session_checkpoint, flight_recorder, app swarm_runtime, SwarmControlRoom, and OperatorChat surfaces.
WP-KERNEL-004-sandbox-vm-execution-research-basis SandboxAdapter, CloudHypervisorAdapter, SandboxModelRuntime, SpawnRequest isolation_tier/worktree_id, and SpawnMeta sandbox fields.
DurableSwarmFrBridge / FanoutSwarmSink / BroadcastSwarmSink pattern already identified for swarm events.
gaps_closed: Closes the gap between a tested SwarmCoordinator and production model sessions that actually load and attach runtimes.
Closes the ledger gap where local/sandbox model spawns can run without sandbox adapter attribution.
Closes the teardown gap where canceling or lease-expiring a swarm session might stop coordination but leave a runtime/model allocation alive.
Closes the observability gap where production swarm events are not durably queryable.
Makes later operator UI work cheaper by giving MT-205 one production command/state seam instead of several ad hoc loaders.

high_roi_additions.item: Factory construction receipt in app startup diagnostics
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Cheap while app state is being wired and gives operators/models an immediate proof of which runtime providers were actually available.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Silent fallback to a fake or missing local runtime.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Existing app diagnostics and Flight Recorder event emission.
high_roi_additions.validation: Startup test asserts a typed provider-capability snapshot is emitted without opening foreground windows.

high_roi_additions.item: No-orphan teardown invariant test across lease expiry, explicit cancel, and duplicate spawn rejection
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Touches the same factory/coordinator path and prevents expensive later memory leaks.
high_roi_additions.addresses: ModelRuntimeState live_runtimes leak and process-ledger START without STOP.
high_roi_additions.reuse: MT-203 teardown seam and process-ledger START/STOP symmetry tests.
high_roi_additions.validation: Focused tests assert attached runtime count, teardown-call count, and ledger START/STOP parity.

high_roi_additions.item: Provider capability snapshot stored on SpawnRequest/session metadata
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Makes MT-206 routing and MT-205 UI decisions deterministic without extra probing.
high_roi_additions.addresses: UI confusion and routing rework from unknown runtime capabilities.
high_roi_additions.validation: Spawned session exposes runtime_binding, isolation_tier, capabilities, and provider in session snapshot.

high_roi_additions.item: Sandbox attribution wired even for fake-adapter tests
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The test seam is already needed and catches the exact metadata gap before live CH runs.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Loss of audit trail for VM-local model execution.
high_roi_additions.validation: Fake adapter test asserts both fields are non-null when Tier3Microvm is selected.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Factory creates a session but fails before registry insert, leaving an orphan model process or Arc.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: CandleRuntime load succeeds, process-ledger START succeeds, then attach_live_runtime fails or duplicate instance rejection happens after the load.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Use one spawn path with reserve/rollback discipline: reserve instance, construct runtime, attach, record START, and register teardown compensations for every failure edge. Duplicate instance rejection must occur before paying model load cost where possible.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Inject failures at each create step and assert teardown/drop count, live_runtimes count, and ledger STOP/FAILED records.

risks_and_concerns.risk: App startup wires a test or null factory into production state.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: A default constructor compiles by using fake workers from MT-203 tests because product dependencies are unavailable in the governance worktree.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Make production factory constructors explicit and typed; test factories must live behind test cfg or named test constructors.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Static grep/compile test rejects Fake/Test factory symbols in non-test production construction.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Sandbox model metadata is omitted from ledger rows.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: Tier3Microvm local sessions run through CH but START rows do not record sandbox_adapter_id or sandbox_internal_id, breaking audit and cleanup.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Require sandbox metadata in the sandbox branch before returning Ready; missing metadata is a typed factory error.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Fake CH adapter test inspects START metadata and teardown STOP metadata.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Long local model loads block the async runtime.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: Large safetensors/GGUF load happens directly on a Tokio worker and stalls IPC, board updates, and reaper tasks.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Use the existing CLI-bridge discipline or spawn_blocking/OS-thread boundary for blocking load work; reaper remains responsive.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Concurrency test starts a slow fake load while the coordinator processes cancel/reaper events.

implementation_notes: Preserve MT-202 as the local model-load authority. Do not fork a second load registry.

ProductionModelSessionFactory should be dependency-injected with ModelRuntimeState, runtime provider registry, ProcessOwnershipLedger, FlightRecorder/SwarmEventSink, and optional SandboxAdapterRegistry.
Local+LlamaCpp+Tier3Microvm should route to SandboxModelRuntime only when isolation_tier == Tier3Microvm; ordinary local llama.cpp remains unchanged.
Teardown must detach/drop the runtime and record STOP even when coordinator cancellation, lease expiry, duplicate spawn, or breaker suppression occurs.
Every production constructor must be quiet: no foreground windows, no focus capture, no unbounded background process.
validator_focus: No fake/test runtime is reachable from production app state.
Every successful spawn has exactly one live runtime and every terminal path detaches/drops it.
START/STOP ledger symmetry holds under success, cancel, lease expiry, duplicate spawn, load failure, and app shutdown.
Sandbox local sessions record sandbox_adapter_id and sandbox_internal_id.
Production factory emits durable, queryable swarm lifecycle events.

lifecycle.readiness_note: Do not mark READY_FOR_VALIDATION without a proper checklist receipt and focused proof logs.

governance_notes: Governance repair 2026-06-02 reconciled packet.json so MT-204..MT-207 are declared contracts, not missing follow-ons.
packet.json now records MT-204 as the active/claimable next follow-on while preserving MT-202 and MT-203 as READY_FOR_VALIDATION pending Integration Validator review.
The visible governance worktree does not include the full product source topology referenced by the research basis; validators must verify against the product worktree before implementation.

minimum_controls: Negative tests prove no runtime survives failed spawn paths.
Production app-state wiring cannot compile with test-only factories.
Ledger START/STOP parity is proven under forced failure scenarios.
Sandbox metadata is mandatory for Tier3Microvm local sessions.
